title: cells claim re-backed by O_EXCL claim file + session-id self-derivation (D1+D3)
action: FREEZE FIRST: run test_lib.mjs + test_bee_cli.mjs green, record output. Per D3: export resolveSessionId({flag}) from claims.mjs — precedence explicit flag -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID env -> null; claim-next keeps --session-id. Per D1 (Δ2-amended): claimCell acquires the claim file via claimCellFile FIRST (typed CLAIMED refusal naming owner session + expiry), then mutates cell JSON. claimCellFile session requirement deliberately relaxed: sessionless single-user claims allowed; releaseClaim owner check handles absent session. CORRECTION (plan-check): NO existing release on cap/unclaim — releaseClaim has exactly one caller today (claim-next unwind, cells.mjs:831). REQUIRED: release the claim file on EVERY claim-clearing transition — capCell, unclaimCell, blockCell, dropCell, reopenCell — else a same-session block/drop->reopen->claim self-refuses CLAIMED for the full TTL. Forked-racer scripts/test_claim_race.mjs: N concurrent cells claim --id X -> exactly 1 winner, N-1 typed CLAIMED; plus a claim->block->reopen->claim same-session round-trip proving no self-refusal. Existing rows byte-unchanged (freeze). Manifest --write + --check.

truths: single-session flow (no env id) still claims successfully
loser refusal names owner and expiry

verify: node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: cells claim --id (bee.mjs handleCellsClaim) is re-backed by the same O_EXCL claim-file gate claim-next already uses (claims.mjs claimCellFile via cells.mjs claimCellCrossSession), so concurrent claimants on one cell now produce exactly one winner and N-1 typed CLAIMED refusals instead of a silent double-claim. Session id resolution is centralized in claims.mjs's new resolveSessionId (explicit flag -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID env -> null), used by both cells claim and cells claim-next; a null/absent session is a legal sessionless claim (claim file omits the session key rather than writing null). Every claim-clearing cell transition (cap/unclaim/block/drop/reopen) now releases the claim file via a new claims.mjs clearClaim helper, closing the same-session self-refusal gap the plan-check correction identified.
deviations: AUTO-ADD (rule 2, missing critical functionality): edited skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/command-registry.mjs + mirror .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs — NOT in the cell's declared `files`. Required to add optional `session-id`/`ttl` properties to the cells.claim registry entry (so the CLI's arg-schema validator does not refuse the new optional flags) and to drop `session-id` from cells.claim-next's `required` list (so it can resolve from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID per D3; the handler itself still functionally requires a session id from one of the two sources). Without this the CLI-level D3 behavior the cell's own action text calls for ('use it in claim-next... existing --session-id keeps working') could not ship — validate() runs before the handler and would refuse the call outright. Not a frozen-judge pattern file (not test/CI/lockfile/config) — no judge hit.

AUTO-ADD (rule 2, missing critical functionality): added 4 new checks to skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs — NOT in the cell's declared `files`. Proves the O_EXCL claim-file gate through the REAL `cells claim`/`cells claim-next` CLI verbs (bee.mjs's handleCellsClaim/handleCellsClaimNext), not only at the lib level: (1) a double `cells claim --id` on the same cell refuses the second call with a typed CLAIMED error naming the owner, (2) a sessionless claim (no --session-id, no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID) still claims cleanly, (3) claim-next resolves --session-id from CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID when the flag is omitted, and refuses clearly when neither source is present. This is the most direct proof of must-have truths 'single-session flow (no env id) still claims successfully' and 'loser refusal names owner and expiry' at the actual CLI surface. FROZEN-JUDGE NOTE: test_bee_cli.mjs matches the 'test sources' judge pattern and is undeclared in this cell's `files` — flagged here explicitly rather than left for a surprise `cells judge` finding; re-run `bee cells judge --id msh-2` to confirm the hit and its scope (additive test rows only, no test deleted/weakened, all pre-existing rows byte-unchanged and still green).

DESIGN NOTE (not a rule 1-4 deviation, recorded for reviewer clarity): the cell's action text says 'claimCell acquires the claim file via claimCellFile first... then mutates cell JSON'. The literal exported `claimCell` function is left UNCHANGED (still a bare, file-less read-check-write) to preserve the pinned W4 unwind test (test_lib.mjs 'claimCellCrossSession: a claimCell THROW after the claim file was created releases the claim file') and claimNextCell's exact tested ordering (claimCellFile succeeds even though the cell has uncapped deps, then claimCell throws) — embedding a second claimCellFile call inside claimCell itself would make claimCellCrossSession's own outer claimCellFile call collide with claimCell's inner one for the SAME session, producing a spurious self-CLAIMED failure instead of the real uncapped-deps error the pinned test asserts. Instead, `cells claim --id` (bee.mjs handleCellsClaim) is re-backed by REUSING the already-tested claimCellCrossSession (the exact function claim-next already uses for its own claim-file-first + cell-mutate sequence), relaxed to accept a nullable sessionId for the sessionless case. From the CLI verb's point of view — the thing the cell is actually titled after — the claim file is acquired before the cell JSON flips, exactly as specified; the bare `claimCell` symbol itself simply stays the internal 'mutate the cell JSON' half it always was.
